engineGuide.h2: BMW X1 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the BMW X1 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The N47D20 powered first-generation X1 sDrive18d and xDrive20d models, but its rear-mounted timing chain became the defining failure of this generation.
compatibleVariants: sDrive18d, sDrive18d M Sport, sDrive20d, sDrive20d M Sport, xDrive20d, xDrive20d M Sport, xDrive23d, xDrive25d
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and guide failure (60,000-100,000 miles)
Swirl flap failure causing intake manifold damage
entries.cta: Get quotes for N47D20 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The B47 replaced the N47 with a revised timing chain design positioned at the front of the engine, largely resolving the catastrophic chain failure issues that plagued its predecessor.
compatibleVariants: sDrive18d, sDrive18d M Sport, sDrive20d, sDrive20d M Sport, xDrive20d, xDrive20d M Sport, xDrive23d, xDrive23d M Sport, xDrive25d, xDrive25d M Sport
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues in short-trip driving
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Mild Hybrid Diesel
entries.history: The B47 in mild hybrid form added a 48V starter-generator to the U11 X1, improving efficiency and reducing turbo lag in the latest generation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 MHEV BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The B37 three-cylinder diesel offered remarkable fuel economy for urban-focused X1 buyers, sharing its modular design with the B38 petrol and B47 diesel families.
commonFailures: Increased noise and vibration at idle (early production)
entries.cta: Get quotes for B37C15 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated N46 was the entry-level petrol engine for first-generation E84 X1, delivering modest power with reasonable reliability for its era.
commonFailures: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for N46B20 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: BMW's first four-cylinder turbocharged petrol engine brought forced induction to the X1 range, offering a compelling blend of power and efficiency.
compatibleVariants: xDrive20i, xDrive20i M Sport, xDrive25i, xDrive25i M Sport, xDrive28i, xDrive28i M Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain guide failure (similar to N47, rear-mounted chain)
VANOS solenoid and camshaft timing issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for N20B20 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The B38 three-cylinder petrol engine debuted in the F48 X1, balancing small-displacement efficiency with surprising refinement for a three-cylinder unit.
compatibleVariants: sDrive18i, sDrive18i M Sport, xDrive18i, xDrive18i M Sport, xDrive25e (PHEV variant)
entries.cta: Get quotes for B38B15 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The B48 replaced the N20 as BMW's four-cylinder turbo petrol, bringing modular architecture, improved thermal management, and significantly better reliability to the X1 range.
compatibleVariants: sDrive20i, sDrive20i M Sport, xDrive20i, xDrive20i M Sport, xDrive25i (late model)
commonFailures: Crankshaft pulley/crankshaft oil seal failure (early B48, SIB110415)
Coolant leaks from expansion tank and hoses
Valve deposit buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
entries.cta: Get quotes for B48B20 BMW X1 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Plug-in Hybrid Petrol
entries.history: The xDrive25e combined the B38 three-cylinder petrol with an electric motor, delivering BMW's first plug-in hybrid X1 with impressive electric-only range and family-friendly practicality.
compatibleVariants: xDrive25e, xDrive25e M Sport, xDrive25e xLine
commonFailures: Electric motor drive unit bearing wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for B38B15 PHEV BMW X1 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: BMW X1 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: BMW X1 N47 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: BMW X1 N47 Timing Chain Failure - Why First-Generation Diesel Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: BMW X1 sDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive23d, xDrive25d (2009-2015, N47D20)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain is positioned at the rear of the N47 engine block, inadequately lubricated. Chain guides become brittle and shatter, allowing the chain to skip teeth or snap completely.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, guides, tensioner, and sprockets - engine remains in car
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure if done before chain rattles. Adds 60k+ miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace bent valves, rebuild head, fit new timing assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if chain slipped without snapping. High labour, uncertain outcome.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N47 Engine (Supply & Fit)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components, fitted, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution. Upgraded chain guides prevent recurrence.

problems.recommendation: If your N47 X1 has over 60,000 miles and the chain rattles on cold start for 1-2 seconds, replace the timing chain preventatively at a specialist. That £1,000-£1,600 investment saves the engine. If the chain has already snapped, a full rebuilt engine is usually cheaper than main dealer cylinder head work - and gives you a warranty.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N47D20 engine prices for your BMW X1

problems.group: BMW X1 B47 EGR Cooler Failure
problems.h4: BMW X1 B47 EGR Cooler Failure - The Most Common F48 Diesel Fault
problems.affectedModels: BMW X1 sDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive25d (2015-2022, B47D20)
problems.rootCause: The exhaust gas recirculation cooler develops internal cracks, allowing coolant to leak into the exhaust system or combustion chambers, potentially causing hydro-lock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler, flush cooling system, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes the immediate fault if caught before coolant enters cylinders.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt B47 Engine (Supply & Fit)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt unit with revised EGR cooler, fitted, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if hydrolock has bent connecting rods - specialist inspection critical.

problems.recommendation: BMW issued a recall on B47 EGR coolers - check if your X1 has outstanding work. If coolant has entered the engine and caused misfires or a no-start condition, do not drive it. Have a specialist borescope the cylinders. Bent rods mean replacement; otherwise a new cooler and oil change may save it.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B47D20 EGR cooler repair and replacement

problems.group: BMW X1 N20 Timing Chain Guide Failure
problems.h4: BMW X1 N20 Timing Chain Guide Failure - Petrol X1's Hidden Weakness
problems.affectedModels: BMW X1 xDrive20i, xDrive25i, xDrive28i (2012-2015, N20B20)
problems.rootCause: Similar to the N47 diesel, the N20 petrol engine places the timing chain at the rear with plastic guides that become brittle and shatter without warning.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner, oil pump drive - engine remains in car
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure. No warning rattle often precedes sudden failure - act proactively.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N20 Engine (Supply & Fit)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt unit with upgraded chain components, fitted, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Chain failure bends valves - full replacement is typically only viable option post-failure.

problems.recommendation: On an N20-powered X1 over 50,000 miles, the £950-£1,500 preventative chain replacement is excellent value. The engine rarely gives audible warning before failure - if you own one, treat this as scheduled maintenance. Post-failure, dealer repair costs often exceed the car's value; a specialist rebuilt engine is the sensible path.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N20B20 engine prices for BMW X1 xDrive28i

problems.group: BMW X1 B47 & N47 Turbocharger Failure
problems.h4: BMW X1 B47 & N47 Turbocharger Failure - Oil Starvation and Carbon Kill Turbos
problems.affectedModels: BMW X1 sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive25d (N47D20 2009-2015, B47D20 2015-2022)
problems.rootCause: Extended oil change intervals or poor-quality oil cause turbo bearing wear. On N47, the front catalytic converter can disintegrate and send debris into the turbo.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace turbo unit, new oil feed lines, fresh oil and filter
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before debris reaches intercooler and engine.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Turbo (Supply & Fit)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply rebuilt engine, new turbo, complete oil system flush, fitted, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if turbo debris has scored bores or damaged bottom end bearings.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a siren noise or see blue smoke from the exhaust on boost, stop driving. A £800-£1,400 turbo replacement at a specialist saves the engine. If the turbo has disintegrated, demand a full oil system flush and borescope inspection - debris kills the replacement turbo quickly. On N47s, also replace the front cat.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 turbo replacement and engine repair

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW X1
finalCta.paragraph: The failures above account for over 80% of BMW X1 engine replacement enquiries we see - timing chains on N47 and N20, EGR coolers on B47, and turbo failures across both diesel families. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK BMW X1 specialists who use upgraded components specifically designed to prevent these failures from recurring. Every rebuilt engine includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, nationwide delivery, and supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW X1 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW X1 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: BMW X1 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDescription: Overhauled replacement unit focusing on failed or worn components identified during strip-down inspection.

engineSizes.h2: BMW X1 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW X1 across all UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

items.title: BMW X1 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre three-cylinder diesel arrived with the F48 second-generation X1 (2015-2022) as the ultra-efficient entry point to the range. Badged sDrive16d, this B37 unit prioritised economy over outright performance and was offered exclusively in front-wheel drive, making it ideal for urban and high-mileage fleet buyers.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X1 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel is the backbone of the X1 diesel range, spanning all three generations. Early E84 models (2009-2015) used the N47D20 - notorious for timing chain failure - while F48 (2015-2022) and U11 (2022-present) generations adopted the improved B47D20. The 2.0-litre diesel powers everything from sDrive18d entry models to high-output xDrive25d performance diesels.
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: BMW X1 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: BMW introduced the 1.5-litre three-cylinder turbo petrol with the F48 X1 (2015-2022) as a genuinely efficient replacement for older naturally aspirated four-cylinder units. The B38 engine in sDrive18i guise delivers 136 hp with a characterful three-cylinder note and strong low-down torque, making it a popular choice for private buyers prioritising running costs over outright pace.
items.cta: Compare BMW X1 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X1 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre four-cylinder turbo petrol powers the sportier end of the X1 petrol range. First-generation E84 models used the N20B20 (2012-2015), which shared the N47's rear-mounted timing chain vulnerability. The B48B20 that followed in F48 (2015-2022) and U11 (2022-present) generations resolved these issues while delivering refined performance in sDrive20i and xDrive20i trims.
items.cta: Compare BMW X1 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: BMW X1 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: BMW X1 1.5L Plug-in Hybrid Engines
items.description: The xDrive25e joined the X1 range during the F48 lifecycle (2020-2022) and continued into the U11 generation (2022-present), combining the B38 three-cylinder petrol engine with an electric motor and high-voltage battery. This plug-in hybrid setup delivers a combined 220 hp and offers up to 30-40 miles of electric-only range, appealing to company car drivers and environmentally conscious buyers seeking the X1's practicality with lower BIK tax liability.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X1 1.5L hybrid engine availability

items.title: BMW X1 2.0L Mild Hybrid Diesel Engines
items.description: The latest U11 generation X1 introduced 48V mild hybrid technology to the 2.0-litre B47 diesel, badged xDrive23d. A belt-integrated starter-generator (BSG) adds a small electric boost during acceleration and enables efficient coasting, improving real-world fuel economy while reducing turbo lag from low revs.
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X1 2.0L mild hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: BMW X1 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your BMW X1 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early E84 diesels to the latest U11 plug-in hybrid.

items.title: BMW X1 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel has powered the majority of UK X1 sales across all three generations, with the N47 driving high replacement demand on E84 cars and the improved B47 dominating F48 and U11 models.
items.descriptor: Diesel has powered the majority of UK X1 sales across all three generations, with the N47 driving high replacement demand on E84 cars and the improved B47 dominating F48 and U11 models.
families: N47D20 - E84 sDrive18d/xDrive20d (2009-2015), timing chain failure drives most replacements
B47D20 - F48 (2015-2022) and U11 (2022-present), EGR cooler failures common
B37C15 - F48 sDrive16d (2015-2022), three-cylinder ultra-economy option
B47D20 MHEV - U11 xDrive23d (2022-present), 48V mild hybrid assistance
foundIn: U11 third generation (2022-present) - B47D20, B47D20 MHEV
knownFor: Excellent real-world fuel economy, especially on motorway runs
N47 timing chain failure between 60,000-100,000 miles (E84 models)
B47 EGR cooler recalls and injector seal leaks (F48 models)
High UK replacement demand on sDrive18d and xDrive20d variants
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: BMW X1 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol X1s are less common in the UK than diesels but cover a wider performance spread - from economical three-cylinder sDrive18i to the punchy xDrive28i with the N20 turbo unit.
items.descriptor: Petrol X1s are less common in the UK than diesels but cover a wider performance spread - from economical three-cylinder sDrive18i to the punchy xDrive28i with the N20 turbo unit.
families: N46B20 - E84 sDrive18i (2009-2015), naturally aspirated entry petrol
N20B20 - E84 xDrive20i/xDrive25i/xDrive28i (2012-2015), timing chain guide vulnerability
B38B15 - F48 sDrive18i/xDrive18i (2015-2022) and U11 (2022-present), three-cylinder turbo
B48B20 - F48 sDrive20i/xDrive20i (2015-2022) and U11 (2022-present), four-cylinder successor to N20
knownFor: Refined four-cylinder performance in xDrive20i and xDrive28i trims
N20 timing chain guide failure (E84, 50,000-90,000 miles) similar to diesel N47 issues
B38 and B48 engines resolved chain problems with improved reliability
Lower UK replacement volume than diesel, but petrol enquiries rising on F48 models
items.cta: Compare BMW X1 Petrol engine prices

items.title: BMW X1 Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The xDrive25e plug-in hybrid arrived on F48 (2020-2022) and continues into U11 (2022-present), combining a 1.5-litre B38 petrol engine with an electric motor and high-voltage battery.
items.descriptor: The xDrive25e plug-in hybrid arrived on F48 (2020-2022) and continues into U11 (2022-present), combining a 1.5-litre B38 petrol engine with an electric motor and high-voltage battery.
families: B38B15 PHEV - three-cylinder petrol with integrated electric motor (xDrive25e)
High-voltage battery (12.0-14.2 kWh depending on generation)
Electric drive unit (rear axle on U11, integrated transmission on F48)
Electric-only range of 30-40 miles from a full charge for urban driving
Petrol engine automatically engages for higher speeds or when battery depletes
Regenerative braking recharges the battery during deceleration
foundIn: xDrive25e, xDrive25e M Sport, xDrive25e xLine (F48 LCI, 2020-2022; U11, 2022-present)
importantNotes: PHEV engine replacement is rare - most powertrain issues involve the hybrid system or battery
Replacement requires specialist hybrid-safe workshop with high-voltage training
Battery condition check is mandatory when replacing the combustion unit
Availability of rebuilt PHEV engines is more limited than standard petrol units
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X1 PHEV engine availability

items.title: BMW X1 Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The U11 generation introduced 48V mild hybrid technology to the 2.0-litre B47 diesel, badged xDrive23d, using a belt-integrated starter-generator (BSG) for efficiency gains.
items.descriptor: The U11 generation introduced 48V mild hybrid technology to the 2.0-litre B47 diesel, badged xDrive23d, using a belt-integrated starter-generator (BSG) for efficiency gains.
families: B47D20 MHEV - 2.0-litre diesel with 48V starter-generator (xDrive23d)
48V lithium-ion battery (small, under bonnet or boot floor)
Belt-integrated starter-generator (replaces standard alternator)
Starter-generator adds up to 11hp electric boost during acceleration
Enables coasting with engine off at motorway speeds to save fuel
Recovers energy during braking to recharge the 48V battery
importantNotes: MHEV systems have minimal impact on engine replacement complexity
48V battery should be inspected and tested during engine swap
Specialist MHEV diagnostic equipment required for post-fit calibration
Low UK replacement volume currently - cars still under manufacturer warranty
items.cta: Enquire about BMW X1 MHEV engine availability

engineYears.h2: BMW X1 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Trace every engine fitted to the BMW X1 from its 2009 launch to the present day, organised by year and generation - helping owners identify their exact unit and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: E84 Launch: Six-Cylinder Petrol & Early N47 Diesel
years.description: The X1 debuted in late 2009 with BMW's modular E84 platform, sharing its rear-drive architecture with the E91 3 Series Touring and launching with an unexpected six-cylinder petrol flagship.
keyChanges: Model launched in European markets (October 2009) with nine initial variants
xDrive28i opened the range with a naturally aspirated 3.0L six-cylinder
xDrive23d offered the most powerful N47 twin-turbo diesel in the line-up
sDrive rear-wheel-drive and xDrive all-wheel-drive configurations both available
Six-speed manual and six-speed automatic transmissions offered
mainEngines: N47D20 - 2.0L Diesel (sDrive18d, xDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive23d)
knownFor: The only X1 generation with a six-cylinder petrol engine option
xDrive28i (N52) delivers a smooth 258hp and a 0-62mph time of 6.8 seconds
Early N47 diesel timing chain issues begin to emerge in later mileage
The first BMW compact SUV - positioned between the 1 Series and X3
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2010 BMW X1 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW X1 xDrive23d engine replacement • BMW X1 N47 timing chain failure • BMW X1 sDrive20d rebuilt engine price
enquiries: BMW X1 N47 timing chain failure
BMW X1 sDrive20d rebuilt engine price

years.description: 2011 brought the N20 four-cylinder turbo petrol - BMW's first modern turbo four - replacing the naturally aspirated six-cylinder across most of the petrol range.
keyChanges: N20B20 2.0L turbo four-cylinder replaces N52 six-cylinder in sDrive20i, xDrive20i, and xDrive28i
sDrive20d EfficientDynamics Edition launched with 161hp and reduced fuel consumption
ZF 8-speed automatic transmission introduced alongside existing 6-speed
Stop-start technology added across the range
xDrive35i with N55 six-cylinder turbo added in late 2012 for some markets
mainEngines: N47D20 - 2.0L Diesel (sDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive23d, xDrive25d)
knownFor: The N20 brought 241hp to the xDrive28i - more power than the N52 it replaced
N20 timing chain guide issues become a major concern in later years
N47 timing chain problems continue to affect diesel variants
EfficientDynamics Edition achieves sub-4.5L/100km fuel economy
years.cta: Compare 2011-2012 BMW X1 engine replacement costs
years.ticker: BMW X1 N20 timing chain replacement • BMW X1 xDrive28i engine rebuild • BMW X1 sDrive20i N20 failure
enquiries: BMW X1 N20 timing chain replacement

years.preview: E84 LCI: N20 Issues Resolved & Turbo Across All Petrol Models
years.description: The 2012-2013 facelift (officially 2014 model year in some markets) upgraded the N20 with revised timing chain components and finally dropped the outdated N46 naturally aspirated four-cylinder.
keyChanges: N20 timing chain guides redesigned with improved material (2014 onwards)
sDrive18i finally receives N20 turbo engine, dropping the N46 naturally aspirated unit
N20 now powers every petrol X1 - from entry sDrive18i to flagship xDrive28i
xDrive28i power increased to 241hp on N20 variant
xDrive25d receives 215hp from a high-output N47 twin-turbo
Last production years for the E84 before F48 replacement
mainEngines: N20B20 - 2.0L Petrol Turbo (sDrive18i, sDrive20i, xDrive20i, xDrive28i)
N47D20 - 2.0L Diesel (sDrive16d, sDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive25d)
N47D20 - 2.0L Diesel Biturbo (xDrive23d, xDrive25d higher output)
knownFor: Post-2014 N20 engines have revised chain guides - much more reliable
sDrive18i gains the turbo N20 - a major upgrade over the sluggish N46
Last chance for rear-wheel-drive X1 before F48 switches to front-drive UKL platform
This generation (2014-2015) is the most recommended E84 variant by specialists
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 BMW X1 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW X1 2014 engine replacement • BMW X1 sDrive18i N20 price • BMW X1 xDrive25d rebuilt engine • BMW X1 2015 timing chain recall

years.preview: F48 Launch: UKL Platform, B-Series Engines Arrive
years.description: The second-generation F48 moved to BMW's UKL front-drive platform, shared with the 2 Series Active Tourer and Mini Countryman - and introduced the modular B-series engine family.
keyChanges: Complete platform shift from rear-drive E84 to front-drive UKL architecture
B38 three-cylinder 1.5L petrol arrives - 136hp in sDrive18i
B48 four-cylinder 2.0L petrol arrives - 192hp in sDrive/xDrive20i, 231hp in xDrive25i
B37 three-cylinder 1.5L diesel arrives - sDrive16d
B47 replaces N47 as the four-cylinder diesel - 150hp in 18d, 190hp in 20d
Eight-speed Aisin automatic replaces ZF unit
mainEngines: B47D20 - 2.0L Diesel (sDrive18d, xDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive25d)
knownFor: B47 diesel resolves the timing chain issues that plagued N47
F48 is significantly roomier than E84 - more practical for families
Front-drive platform means very different driving character from first generation
B38 three-cylinder is the first three-cylinder engine ever fitted to an X1
years.ticker: BMW X1 F48 engine replacement • BMW X1 B47 EGR cooler recall • BMW X1 sDrive18i B38 rebuild • BMW X1 2016 diesel engine cost
enquiries: BMW X1 B47 EGR cooler recall

years.preview: F48 LCI: Power Increases & Tech Updates
years.description: The F48 facelift arrived in 2019 for the 2020 model year, bringing subtle styling updates, more power from the B-series engines, and improved standard equipment.
keyChanges: B38 engine updated to B38A15M1 - power increased to 140hp
B48B20 receives power bump - 192hp in 20i, 231hp in 25i remains
New 7-speed dual-clutch transmission for front-drive petrol variants
xDrive25e plug-in hybrid (B38 + electric motor, 220hp combined) arrives 2020
Larger standard touchscreen with iDrive 7 interface
Apple CarPlay and Android Auto become standard
mainEngines: B37C15 - 1.5L Diesel (sDrive16d, discontinued in some markets)
B47D20 - 2.0L Diesel (sDrive18d, xDrive18d, sDrive20d, xDrive20d, xDrive25d)
knownFor: xDrive25e plug-in hybrid offers 30+ miles electric range
B47 EGR cooler failures lead to a major recall (warranty extension)
Last petrol and diesel-only X1 before more aggressive electrification
F48 remains the most common X1 on UK roads - high replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2022 BMW X1 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW X1 B47 EGR cooler replacement • BMW X1 xDrive25e hybrid engine • BMW X1 2020 engine recall • BMW X1 sDrive18i B38 price
enquiries: BMW X1 B47 EGR cooler replacement

years.preview: Present - U11 Launch: Third Generation, Full Electrification, MHEV & PHEV
years.description: The third-generation U11 X1 arrived for the 2023 model year on an evolved front-drive platform, bringing mild-hybrid diesel technology, a revised PHEV, and pure electric (iX1) for the first time.
keyChanges: New platform with more aggressive styling and larger grille
B47 diesel now available with 48V mild-hybrid assistance in xDrive23d (211hp)
xDrive25e PHEV updated with larger battery and improved electric range
iX1 pure electric variant launched (not an engine - electric drive unit)
Curved display with iDrive 9 debuts in X1
xDrive23d (MHEV) becomes the sole diesel option in some markets
mainEngines: iX1 - Pure Electric (front and rear motors, no combustion engine)
knownFor: First X1 generation with mild-hybrid diesel (48V BSG system)
xDrive23d combines B47 diesel with electric boost for 211hp
PHEV range increases significantly over F48 xDrive25e
The most technologically advanced X1 ever, but also the most complex for used buyers
years.cta: Compare 2023-present BMW X1 engine replacement options

faq.h2: BMW X1 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a BMW X1 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A BMW X1 rebuilt engine typically costs £1,200-£4,500 supply-only depending on engine code, with B47 diesels at the higher end and N47 units starting lower. Supply & fit adds £400-£800.
keyPoints: B47 diesel (F48/U11, 2015-present): £1,500-£4,500 supply-only
B38/B48 petrol: £1,300-£3,600 supply-only
Dealer replacement costs 40-60% more than specialist prices
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMW X1 engine prices by your exact variant

items.question: Is the BMW X1 N47 timing chain failure really that common?
items.answer: Yes, the N47 diesel in 2009-2015 X1 models has a well-documented timing chain failure risk between 60,000-100,000 miles. BMW acknowledged this internally and issued guidance for dealers.
keyPoints: Chain mounted at rear of engine with inadequate lubrication
Failure can destroy the engine with little audible warning
Preventative replacement costs £1,000-£1,600 at a specialist
Post-failure replacement typically £2,800-£4,000 for rebuilt unit
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 N47 timing chain replacement and engine rebuild

items.question: Which BMW X1 engines should I avoid when buying used?
items.answer: The N47 diesel (E84, 2009-2015) and N20 petrol (E84, 2012-2015) share timing chain vulnerabilities. The B47 diesel (2015+) resolved these issues and is significantly more reliable.
keyPoints: N47 diesel - timing chain failure risk, most avoided by informed buyers
N20 petrol - same chain design flaw, affects xDrive20i/28i models
B47 diesel - recommended, EGR cooler recall but no chain issues
B48 petrol (2015+) - solid reliability, few major reported failures
items.cta: Find a reconditioned B47 BMW X1 engine with upgraded components

items.question: How long do BMW X1 engines last?
items.answer: A well-maintained BMW X1 engine typically lasts 150,000-200,000 miles. The B47 diesel can exceed 200k with proper servicing, while N47 units often fail earlier due to timing chain issues.
keyPoints: B47/B48 B-series engines: 180,000-220,000 miles with good maintenance
N47/N20 engines: chain failure often limits lifespan to 100,000-150,000 miles
Regular oil changes every 8,000-10,000 miles critical for longevity
Preventative timing chain replacement adds 60,000+ miles to N47 lifespan
items.cta: Check availability of low-mileage rebuilt BMW X1 engines

items.question: What causes BMW X1 B47 EGR cooler failure?
items.answer: The B47 diesel EGR cooler can crack internally, allowing coolant to leak into the exhaust system. BMW recalled over 800,000 vehicles globally, including X1 models, due to fire risk.
keyPoints: Glycol coolant escapes from defective cooler into exhaust
Combined with soot and heat creates fire risk in extreme cases
Affects N47, N57 and B47 diesel engines across BMW range
BMW used an improved cooler from a different supplier from 2022
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 B47 EGR cooler replacement and engine inspection

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage BMW X1?
items.answer: On a 2009-2015 E84 X1 worth £3,000-£6,000, a £2,800-£4,000 rebuilt engine often makes sense if the car is otherwise sound. On newer F48 models, replacement is almost always worthwhile.
keyPoints: E84 X1 (2009-2015): borderline decision - depends on body and chassis condition
F48 X1 (2015-2022): replacement is cost-effective, car worth £8,000-£15,000
Rebuilt engine includes 12-month warranty and upgraded components
Specialist replacement costs half what a main dealer charges
items.cta: Compare BMW X1 engine rebuild vs replacement costs for your model year

items.question: Are BMW X1 petrol engines more reliable than diesels?
items.answer: Modern B38/B48 petrol engines (2015+) are generally more reliable than N47 diesels, with fewer catastrophic failure modes. However, older N20 petrol engines share the N47's timing chain vulnerability.
keyPoints: B38/B48 (2015+): very reliable, minor injector and coolant leak issues
N20 (2012-2015): timing chain guide failure risk - avoid or budget for replacement
N46 (2009-2015): robust but underpowered, valve stem seal wear common
Petrol engines avoid EGR cooler and DPF diesel-specific problems
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW X1 petrol engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: What is the labour cost to replace a BMW X1 engine?
items.answer: Specialist labour for BMW X1 engine replacement typically costs £400-£800, covering 8-12 hours of work. Main dealers charge £1,200-£2,000 for the same job.
keyPoints: Independent BMW specialist: £50-£80 per hour, 8-12 hours total
Main dealer: £150-£250 per hour, same labour time
Supply & fit packages typically add £400-£800 to engine price
Fitting includes coolant, oil, filter and basic gaskets
items.cta: Find BMW X1 supply & fit engine deals with UK-wide fitting network

items.question: Does a rebuilt BMW X1 engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Every rebuilt BMW X1 engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Premium suppliers offer 24-month coverage for B-series engines.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty is industry standard for rebuilt units
24-month warranties available on B47/B48 reconditioned engines
Warranty covers parts and defects - labour varies by supplier
Used engines typically carry 30-90 day warranty only
items.cta: Compare BMW X1 reconditioned engines with extended warranty options

items.question: How do I know which engine code my BMW X1 has?
items.answer: Check your BMW X1's VIN plate under the bonnet or in the driver's door shut - the engine code is printed as an 8-11 character code like N47D20C or B47D20. You can also enter your registration above.
keyPoints: VIN plate shows engine code (Motor/Mot) - typically 8-11 characters
Registration lookup above identifies exact engine code instantly
Service history documents list engine code and variant details
Common X1 codes: N47D20, B47D20, B48B20, B38B15, N20B20
items.cta: Enter your registration above to identify your BMW X1 engine code instantly
